sections.paragraph:
(from the root פָּזַז No. 1 ), prop. adj. purified, pure, an epithet of gold, Son 5:11 hence purified, pure gold, Psa 21:4 Lam 4:2 Isa 13:12. It is distinguished from common gold, Psal. 19:11 119:127 Pro 8:19. Rosenmüller (Bibl. Alterthumsk. iv. page 49) prefers rendering it solid gold, comparing ܦܰܙܝܺܙܐܳ hard, heavy; but in an uncertain matter I would not desert the authority of the Book of Chronicles: [the authority of inspired Scripture is of course absolute, nothing can come into competition with it]: (see the root פָּזַז ).

I.
